widgetData.source_sentence: Job Title: Architectural Designer Job Description: Job Summary Architectural Designer located in Versailles, KY for a growing Architecture firm. This is a direct hire opportunity. APPLY NOW! Required Qualifications Bachelor's or Master's degree in Architecture Must have at least one (1) year of experience completing architectural designs for commercial and industrial projects Proficiency in AutoCAD Architecture Preferred Registered Architect (RA), Licensed Architect (LA) location: Versailles, Kentucky job type: Permanent salary: $80,000 - 90,000 per year work hours: 8am to 4pm education: Bachelors Responsibilities Create architectural designs using provided drawings and hand sketches Research, program, plan, design, and administer projects, applying knowledge of architectural design Coordinate and integrate engineering elements into a unified design prepare drawings and specification documents through all phases of a project. Assist in research and coordination of materials and products for project specifications Qualifications Experience level: ExperiencedMinimum 2 years of experienceEducation: Bachelors (required) Skills ArchitectureAutoCAD Equal Opportunity Employer: Race, Color, Religion, Sex, Sexual Orientation, Gender Identity, National Origin, Age, Genetic Information, Disability, Protected Veteran Status, or any other legally protected group status. At Randstad, we welcome people of all abilities and want to ensure that our hiring and interview process meets the needs of all applicants. If you require a reasonable accommodation to make your application or interview experience a great one, please contact HRsupport@randstadusa.com. Pay offered to a successful candidate will be based on several factors including the candidate's education, work experience, work location, specific job duties, certifications, etc. In addition, Randstad offers a comprehensive benefits package, including health, an incentive and recognition program, and 401K contribution (all benefits are based on eligibility). Applications accepted on ongoing basis until filled.

widgetData.source_sentence: Job Title: Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA) Job Description: Job Details Job Location: Rowland Heights, CA Salary Range: Undisclosed DescriptionSalary: $74,000-$79,000 (dependent on experience)ACES is driven to elevate the standards in the treatment of autism. Our team of Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) clinicians is deeply committed to helping children with autism and related disorders reach their fullest potential through home and clinic-based services. ACES is the only autism provider who is nationally recognized for quality care as part of Aetna/CVS's Institute of Quality. We are also an award-winning workplace where you can grow your career, collaborate with a supportive team, and make a lasting impact on the lives of people in your community. What You'll Do: Design, develop and implement a wide range of ABA programs for children with autism and other developmental disabilities Collaborate with families and other clinicians to tailor ABA programs to specific client needs Supervise and mentor Behavior Technicians delivering direct services Evaluate and update client progress reports via industry-leading data collection software Collaborate with a cross-functional team dedicated to ACES' mission What You'll Love: Referral bonus of $2000 total ($1000 after the referred employee has been with ACES for 6 months and $1000 after the referred employee has been with ACES for 12 months) Competitive total compensation with monthly performance incentive Career and compensation growth opportunity from Supervisor to Area Manager then Area Director and beyond Up to 21 paid days off each year: 8 holidays, 10 paid days, 3 sick days 12 free, in-house CEUs (one a month) 401k retirement plan with company match and free financial planning Medical, Dental, Vision insurance, coverage within 30 days of your start Company-paid life insurance with buy-up option Access to Identity and Legal shield services Company-provided laptop Free Gympass membership Flexible, uplifting and supportive work environment, including company-sponsored social events Opportunities for relocation assistance, student loan reimbursement and sign-on bonus for qualified applicants QualificationsWhat We're Looking For: Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA) in good standing Proven experience developing and implementing client programs (preferably in the ABA industry) Enthusiastic leaders who enjoy training, managing and supporting entry-level direct care staff Must pass a criminal background check, TB test, and have or receive immunities to MMR/VZV Additional Information ACES is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, disability, age or covered veteran status. To find out more about how ACES uses employee data for business purposes, see ourEmployee Privacy Policy. If you are having any issues with submitting your application, please reach out to us directly at careers@acesaba.com If you require alternative methods of application or screening, you must approach the employer directly to request this as Indeed is not responsible for the employer's application process.PandoLogic. Keywords: Behavior Analyst, Location: Rowland Heights, CA - 91748

widgetData.source_sentence: Job Title: Associate Attorney Job Description: Uncommon Upward Mobility in Fort Lauderdale with Small Firm Culture & Big Firm ResourcesGalloway, Johnson, Tompkins, Burr & Smith, PLC seeks an attorney with 3 - 7 years of defense litigation experience to join our quickly expanding team in Fort Lauderdale handling: insurance defense, auto and premises liability, construction defect cases, 3rd party litigation, breach of contract, constitutional defense, trucking and transportation litigation. We are a growing, AV rated, full-service litigation firm with 13 offices in 7 states and extensive resources for continued expansion in Texas, Louisiana, Mississippi, Missouri, Alabama, Georgia, and Florida. Our unique firm culture promotes a collaborative and flexible environment. We offer an excellent opportunity for upward mobility, including a generous compensation package, significant year-end bonuses, and a wide selection of benefits and perks. Applicants must have some independent experience taking depositions, attending hearings and preparing client reports. Want to know more? Please contact Michelle Bech at mbech@gallowaylawfirm.com for more details. All inquiries are strictly confidential. You may also visit us at www.gallowaylawfirm.com.
sentences: Associate Attorney Galloway, Johnson, Tompkins, Burr & Smith, PLC seeks an attorney with 3 - 7 years of defense litigation experience to join our quickly expanding team in Fort Lauderdale handling insurance defense, auto and premises liability, construction defect cases, 3rd party litigation, breach of contract, constitutional defense, trucking and transportation litigation. I have 5 years of experience as an associate attorney at a mid-sized law firm, where I worked on a variety of defense litigation cases, including insurance defense, auto liability, and breach of contract. My experience includes taking depositions, attending hearings, preparing client reports, and collaborating with colleagues on trial strategies. Additionally, I have experience handling multiple cases from intake to resolution, managing client relationships, and maintaining accurate records and files. Strong technical skills include proficiency in Westlaw and LexisNexis, experience with document management software, and proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. I also possess strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills, as well as the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple tasks and deadlines. Notable projects include serving as lead attorney on a complex construction defect case, where I successfully negotiated a settlement with the plaintiff's counsel and ensured a favorable outcome for the client. I also worked on a high-profile premises liability case, where I conducted extensive discovery, took the deposition of the plaintiff's key witness, and ultimately obtained a defense verdict through trial. I am certified in Florida Trial Techniques and Procedures, and I have completed a training program in Alternative Dispute Resolution through the American Bar Association. I am also admitted to practice in the state of Florida and have a valid Florida Bar membership. I earned my Juris Doctor (J.D.) degree from the University of Florida Levin College of Law, where I graduated cum laude and was a member of the Florida Law Review.

widgetData.source_sentence: Job Title: Business Development Associate Job Description: At Northspyre, you’ll join the incredible journey of transforming the way modern real estate teams manage complex projects. Leveraging data, automation and artificial intelligence, Northspyre helps facilitate faster, more predictable outcomes on complex real estate projects. In 5 years, our platform has already facilitated more than $125 billion of complex projects across the United States. We are a passionate, collaborative, and diverse team who are eager to roll-up our sleeves and make an impact at a startup that has the confidence and backing of some of the same investors who backed other revolutionary, industry transforming companies like SpaceX, Tesla, and Airbnb. Help us build software that builds the world! About The Opportunity We are rapidly expanding and looking for highly motivated, recent graduates/early career professionals to help us with our business development initiatives. Participants in our BDA internship program will undergo intensive sales training, learn the foundations of the real estate industry, and develop their business skills over the course of this 12-week program. Highly successful interns may be offered a full-time role at the end of the program. As a technology-forward company, we welcome anyone interested in breaking into the real estate technology space and is able to work remotely with our dynamic team. Participants will be compensated for the duration of the program. What’s in it for you? Hands-on experience working in a high-paced software sales environment. Learn the ins and outs of business development for venture backed SaaS companies. Learn new trends in the real estate tech space. Room to grow, the best performers in our program will have an opportunity to continue working with us. Build your foundation for future roles in business development, sales, account management, or business operations! In this role, you’ll get to: Learn about real estate projects and how they are delivered. Help real estate project teams across the country leverage sophisticated technologies like automation and proactive intelligence to solve friction points and problems in their current workflows Research and assist with prospecting potential customers Support our team throughout the entire sales cycle via calls, web-demos and occasionally in-person meetings Accurately manage activities, pipeline and your portfolio of clients within the CRM system Receive mentorship and guidance through the program We are looking for people who have / are: Recent college graduate or early career professional interested in the real estate technology industry (Bachelor’s degree required, GPA 3.2+) Excellent communication skills (written and verbal). High emotional intelligence and are able to read the emotions of others effectively. Results and detail-oriented. Ability to build rapport with clients. Not afraid to make cold calls and speak to unfamiliar people on a daily basis. Strong interpersonal and negotiation skills. Desire and passion to be the best at what you do. Please only apply if you are a high-performer and you are willing to work hard to get the most out of this program. We will be investing time with you to give you the foundational knowledge and training to help you jump start your career. We look for team members that are excited to build the future. You’re the type of person that wants to join us on this challenging journey of transforming the real estate development industry. Benefits And Perks (Full-Time Employees) In addition to a competitive salary, we are offering a meaningful stock option packageComprehensive Medical, Dental, and Vision. Multiple plan options, including a plan option for 100% employer-paid premium for individual coverage for full-time employees.401k Match to help you save for your future (Fully vested after 6 mo eligibility period)Unlimited Vacation, Paid Sick Leave, and major US Holidays$45 monthly stipend towards ClassPass (fitness/spa)Awesome company swag!Free access to Health AdvocateRemote-first work environment -we have an office in NYC, but our teammates are all over the United States and around the worldYou will be part of a small, professional fast-moving team with minimal supervisionAnnual team gatherings Top Three Reasons to Join #1: It’s the Right Time – We’ve already facilitated more than $125 billion of complex projects across the United States #2. Northspyre has the right product – Our technology empowers modern real estate teams to build leveraging data, automation and artificial intelligence to get to faster, more predictable outcomes on complex projects. #3. Northspyre celebrates a culture of creativity – We strive to have a sense of community within Northspyre that encourages diverse viewpoints, openness, and fostering an environment in which employees feel comfortable bringing their whole authentic selves to work. We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates, so please don’t hesitate to apply — we’d love to hear from you.
sentences: As a seasoned Cardiac Catheterization Technician, I bring a strong background in providing high-quality patient care and expertise in operating radiological equipment. With over 3 years of experience in the Cardiac Catheterization Laboratory, I have honed my skills in assisting with diagnostic procedures, monitoring patient conditions, and maintaining equipment. My current role as a senior technician has allowed me to develop leadership skills and mentor junior team members. My technical skills include: CRT, Fluoroscopy, RCIS certification, and proficiency in various software programs used for patient data management and radiological equipment operation. Additionally, I possess excellent communication skills, ensuring seamless collaboration with healthcare professionals and patients. My expertise in patient safety, quality improvement initiatives, and emergency response protocols has been a valuable asset to the Cardiac Catheterization team. Notable projects include: Leading a team to implement a new fluoroscopy protocol, resulting in a 20% reduction in radiation exposure for patients, and collaborating with the Assistant Director to develop a comprehensive equipment maintenance program, ensuring 99% uptime for critical radiological equipment. These projects have significantly enhanced the Cardiac Catheterization Laboratory's efficiency and safety standards. Certifications include: RCIS (Registered Cardiovascular Invasive Specialist) from the Cardiovascular Credentialing International (CCI) organization, demonstrating my expertise in cardiovascular invasive procedures. I am also certified in Basic Life Support (BLS) for healthcare professionals. I hold a degree in Cardiovascular Technology (CVT) from the Commission on Accreditation of Allied Health Education Programs (CAAHEP) accredited program at the University of Texas Health Science Center, where I completed a 2-year training program in radiologic technology and cardiovascular invasive procedures.

As a Business Development Associate at a startup specializing in commercial real estate, I gained hands-on experience in software sales and developed a strong foundation in business development for venture-backed SaaS companies. I worked closely with senior sales leaders to identify and pursue new business opportunities, and consistently met or exceeded quarterly sales targets. My time in this role also allowed me to build a comprehensive understanding of the commercial real estate industry, including market trends and best practices. I possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build rapport with clients and effectively negotiate sales agreements. Proficiency in CRM software, data analysis, and project management tools, such as Salesforce, Google Analytics, and Asana, enables me to accurately manage activities, pipeline, and client portfolios. Additionally, I have a strong understanding of sales methodologies, including the Challenger model and the AIDA framework. One notable project I spearheaded was the implementation of a sales strategy for a residential real estate developer, resulting in a 25% increase in sales conversions within a six-month period. I also assisted in the development of a sales enablement program for a commercial real estate broker, which led to a 30% increase in deal closings within the same timeframe. I hold a Certified Real Estate Associate (CREA) designation from the National Association of Realtors, demonstrating my commitment to staying up-to-date on industry trends and best practices. I am also a Certified Sales Professional (CSP) through the National Association of Sales Professionals, showcasing my expertise in sales and customer engagement. I hold a Bachelor of Science degree in Business Administration with a concentration in Marketing from the University of Southern California (USC), graduating in the top 10% of my class with a cumulative GPA of 3.6.

As a seasoned information technology professional with a proven track record in NetSuite administration, I bring over 6 years of experience in managing and implementing NetSuite solutions for various clients. My expertise spans across multiple functional modules, including Manufacturing, Wholesale, Financials, and Inventory management, with a strong focus on optimizing business processes and improving operational efficiency. Prior to my current position, I served as a NetSuite Administrator at a leading E-commerce company, where I successfully implemented NetSuite's SuiteCommerce Advanced platform and streamlined their order management and inventory processes. My technical skills are centered around NetSuite, with expertise in SuiteScripting, SuiteFoundation, and RF Smart Commerce. I also have experience with SPS Commerce and have a solid understanding of integration with other systems. I possess excellent problem-solving skills, with the ability to analyze complex business requirements and design customized solutions to meet them. One of my most notable projects was a complete NetSuite overhaul for a mid-sized manufacturer, which involved migrating their existing system to NetSuite 2019 and implementing SuiteCommerce Advanced. This project resulted in a significant reduction in operational costs and improved customer satisfaction. Another notable project was assisting a large distributor in implementing NetSuite's Warehouse Management System (WMS) and optimizing their inventory management processes. I hold a SuiteFoundation Certification and am well-versed in NetSuite best practices and implementation methodologies. I am a certified SuiteFoundation Professional and have a strong passion for staying up-to-date with the latest NetSuite features and technologies. I hold a Bachelor's degree in Business Administration from the University of Utah, with a focus on Information Systems and Technology.

widget.source_sentence: Job Title: Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA) Job Description: Job Details Job Location: Rowland Heights, CA Salary Range: Undisclosed DescriptionSalary: $74,000-$79,000 (dependent on experience)ACES is driven to elevate the standards in the treatment of autism. Our team of Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) clinicians is deeply committed to helping children with autism and related disorders reach their fullest potential through home and clinic-based services. ACES is the only autism provider who is nationally recognized for quality care as part of Aetna/CVS's Institute of Quality. We are also an award-winning workplace where you can grow your career, collaborate with a supportive team, and make a lasting impact on the lives of people in your community. What You'll Do: Design, develop and implement a wide range of ABA programs for children with autism and other developmental disabilities Collaborate with families and other clinicians to tailor ABA programs to specific client needs Supervise and mentor Behavior Technicians delivering direct services Evaluate and update client progress reports via industry-leading data collection software Collaborate with a cross-functional team dedicated to ACES' mission What You'll Love: Referral bonus of $2000 total ($1000 after the referred employee has been with ACES for 6 months and $1000 after the referred employee has been with ACES for 12 months) Competitive total compensation with monthly performance incentive Career and compensation growth opportunity from Supervisor to Area Manager then Area Director and beyond Up to 21 paid days off each year: 8 holidays, 10 paid days, 3 sick days 12 free, in-house CEUs (one a month) 401k retirement plan with company match and free financial planning Medical, Dental, Vision insurance, coverage within 30 days of your start Company-paid life insurance with buy-up option Access to Identity and Legal shield services Company-provided laptop Free Gympass membership Flexible, uplifting and supportive work environment, including company-sponsored social events Opportunities for relocation assistance, student loan reimbursement and sign-on bonus for qualified applicants QualificationsWhat We're Looking For: Board Certified Behavior Analyst (BCBA) in good standing Proven experience developing and implementing client programs (preferably in the ABA industry) Enthusiastic leaders who enjoy training, managing and supporting entry-level direct care staff Must pass a criminal background check, TB test, and have or receive immunities to MMR/VZV Additional Information ACES is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, disability, age or covered veteran status. To find out more about how ACES uses employee data for business purposes, see ourEmployee Privacy Policy. If you are having any issues with submitting your application, please reach out to us directly at careers@acesaba.com If you require alternative methods of application or screening, you must approach the employer directly to request this as Indeed is not responsible for the employer's application process.PandoLogic. Keywords: Behavior Analyst, Location: Rowland Heights, CA - 91748

widget.source_sentence: Job Title: Associate Attorney Job Description: Uncommon Upward Mobility in Fort Lauderdale with Small Firm Culture & Big Firm ResourcesGalloway, Johnson, Tompkins, Burr & Smith, PLC seeks an attorney with 3 - 7 years of defense litigation experience to join our quickly expanding team in Fort Lauderdale handling: insurance defense, auto and premises liability, construction defect cases, 3rd party litigation, breach of contract, constitutional defense, trucking and transportation litigation. We are a growing, AV rated, full-service litigation firm with 13 offices in 7 states and extensive resources for continued expansion in Texas, Louisiana, Mississippi, Missouri, Alabama, Georgia, and Florida. Our unique firm culture promotes a collaborative and flexible environment. We offer an excellent opportunity for upward mobility, including a generous compensation package, significant year-end bonuses, and a wide selection of benefits and perks. Applicants must have some independent experience taking depositions, attending hearings and preparing client reports. Want to know more? Please contact Michelle Bech at mbech@gallowaylawfirm.com for more details. All inquiries are strictly confidential. You may also visit us at www.gallowaylawfirm.com.
sentences: Associate Attorney Galloway, Johnson, Tompkins, Burr & Smith, PLC seeks an attorney with 3 - 7 years of defense litigation experience to join our quickly expanding team in Fort Lauderdale handling insurance defense, auto and premises liability, construction defect cases, 3rd party litigation, breach of contract, constitutional defense, trucking and transportation litigation. I have 5 years of experience as an associate attorney at a mid-sized law firm, where I worked on a variety of defense litigation cases, including insurance defense, auto liability, and breach of contract. My experience includes taking depositions, attending hearings, preparing client reports, and collaborating with colleagues on trial strategies. Additionally, I have experience handling multiple cases from intake to resolution, managing client relationships, and maintaining accurate records and files. Strong technical skills include proficiency in Westlaw and LexisNexis, experience with document management software, and proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. I also possess strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills, as well as the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple tasks and deadlines. Notable projects include serving as lead attorney on a complex construction defect case, where I successfully negotiated a settlement with the plaintiff's counsel and ensured a favorable outcome for the client. I also worked on a high-profile premises liability case, where I conducted extensive discovery, took the deposition of the plaintiff's key witness, and ultimately obtained a defense verdict through trial. I am certified in Florida Trial Techniques and Procedures, and I have completed a training program in Alternative Dispute Resolution through the American Bar Association. I am also admitted to practice in the state of Florida and have a valid Florida Bar membership. I earned my Juris Doctor (J.D.) degree from the University of Florida Levin College of Law, where I graduated cum laude and was a member of the Florida Law Review.

widget.source_sentence: Job Title: Business Development Associate Job Description: At Northspyre, you’ll join the incredible journey of transforming the way modern real estate teams manage complex projects. Leveraging data, automation and artificial intelligence, Northspyre helps facilitate faster, more predictable outcomes on complex real estate projects. In 5 years, our platform has already facilitated more than $125 billion of complex projects across the United States. We are a passionate, collaborative, and diverse team who are eager to roll-up our sleeves and make an impact at a startup that has the confidence and backing of some of the same investors who backed other revolutionary, industry transforming companies like SpaceX, Tesla, and Airbnb. Help us build software that builds the world! About The Opportunity We are rapidly expanding and looking for highly motivated, recent graduates/early career professionals to help us with our business development initiatives. Participants in our BDA internship program will undergo intensive sales training, learn the foundations of the real estate industry, and develop their business skills over the course of this 12-week program. Highly successful interns may be offered a full-time role at the end of the program. As a technology-forward company, we welcome anyone interested in breaking into the real estate technology space and is able to work remotely with our dynamic team. Participants will be compensated for the duration of the program. What’s in it for you? Hands-on experience working in a high-paced software sales environment. Learn the ins and outs of business development for venture backed SaaS companies. Learn new trends in the real estate tech space. Room to grow, the best performers in our program will have an opportunity to continue working with us. Build your foundation for future roles in business development, sales, account management, or business operations! In this role, you’ll get to: Learn about real estate projects and how they are delivered. Help real estate project teams across the country leverage sophisticated technologies like automation and proactive intelligence to solve friction points and problems in their current workflows Research and assist with prospecting potential customers Support our team throughout the entire sales cycle via calls, web-demos and occasionally in-person meetings Accurately manage activities, pipeline and your portfolio of clients within the CRM system Receive mentorship and guidance through the program We are looking for people who have / are: Recent college graduate or early career professional interested in the real estate technology industry (Bachelor’s degree required, GPA 3.2+) Excellent communication skills (written and verbal). High emotional intelligence and are able to read the emotions of others effectively. Results and detail-oriented. Ability to build rapport with clients. Not afraid to make cold calls and speak to unfamiliar people on a daily basis. Strong interpersonal and negotiation skills. Desire and passion to be the best at what you do. Please only apply if you are a high-performer and you are willing to work hard to get the most out of this program. We will be investing time with you to give you the foundational knowledge and training to help you jump start your career. We look for team members that are excited to build the future. You’re the type of person that wants to join us on this challenging journey of transforming the real estate development industry. Benefits And Perks (Full-Time Employees) In addition to a competitive salary, we are offering a meaningful stock option packageComprehensive Medical, Dental, and Vision. Multiple plan options, including a plan option for 100% employer-paid premium for individual coverage for full-time employees.401k Match to help you save for your future (Fully vested after 6 mo eligibility period)Unlimited Vacation, Paid Sick Leave, and major US Holidays$45 monthly stipend towards ClassPass (fitness/spa)Awesome company swag!Free access to Health AdvocateRemote-first work environment -we have an office in NYC, but our teammates are all over the United States and around the worldYou will be part of a small, professional fast-moving team with minimal supervisionAnnual team gatherings Top Three Reasons to Join #1: It’s the Right Time – We’ve already facilitated more than $125 billion of complex projects across the United States #2. Northspyre has the right product – Our technology empowers modern real estate teams to build leveraging data, automation and artificial intelligence to get to faster, more predictable outcomes on complex projects. #3. Northspyre celebrates a culture of creativity – We strive to have a sense of community within Northspyre that encourages diverse viewpoints, openness, and fostering an environment in which employees feel comfortable bringing their whole authentic selves to work. We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates, so please don’t hesitate to apply — we’d love to hear from you.
sentences: As a seasoned Cardiac Catheterization Technician, I bring a strong background in providing high-quality patient care and expertise in operating radiological equipment. With over 3 years of experience in the Cardiac Catheterization Laboratory, I have honed my skills in assisting with diagnostic procedures, monitoring patient conditions, and maintaining equipment. My current role as a senior technician has allowed me to develop leadership skills and mentor junior team members. My technical skills include: CRT, Fluoroscopy, RCIS certification, and proficiency in various software programs used for patient data management and radiological equipment operation. Additionally, I possess excellent communication skills, ensuring seamless collaboration with healthcare professionals and patients. My expertise in patient safety, quality improvement initiatives, and emergency response protocols has been a valuable asset to the Cardiac Catheterization team. Notable projects include: Leading a team to implement a new fluoroscopy protocol, resulting in a 20% reduction in radiation exposure for patients, and collaborating with the Assistant Director to develop a comprehensive equipment maintenance program, ensuring 99% uptime for critical radiological equipment. These projects have significantly enhanced the Cardiac Catheterization Laboratory's efficiency and safety standards. Certifications include: RCIS (Registered Cardiovascular Invasive Specialist) from the Cardiovascular Credentialing International (CCI) organization, demonstrating my expertise in cardiovascular invasive procedures. I am also certified in Basic Life Support (BLS) for healthcare professionals. I hold a degree in Cardiovascular Technology (CVT) from the Commission on Accreditation of Allied Health Education Programs (CAAHEP) accredited program at the University of Texas Health Science Center, where I completed a 2-year training program in radiologic technology and cardiovascular invasive procedures.

As a Business Development Associate at a startup specializing in commercial real estate, I gained hands-on experience in software sales and developed a strong foundation in business development for venture-backed SaaS companies. I worked closely with senior sales leaders to identify and pursue new business opportunities, and consistently met or exceeded quarterly sales targets. My time in this role also allowed me to build a comprehensive understanding of the commercial real estate industry, including market trends and best practices. I possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build rapport with clients and effectively negotiate sales agreements. Proficiency in CRM software, data analysis, and project management tools, such as Salesforce, Google Analytics, and Asana, enables me to accurately manage activities, pipeline, and client portfolios. Additionally, I have a strong understanding of sales methodologies, including the Challenger model and the AIDA framework. One notable project I spearheaded was the implementation of a sales strategy for a residential real estate developer, resulting in a 25% increase in sales conversions within a six-month period. I also assisted in the development of a sales enablement program for a commercial real estate broker, which led to a 30% increase in deal closings within the same timeframe. I hold a Certified Real Estate Associate (CREA) designation from the National Association of Realtors, demonstrating my commitment to staying up-to-date on industry trends and best practices. I am also a Certified Sales Professional (CSP) through the National Association of Sales Professionals, showcasing my expertise in sales and customer engagement. I hold a Bachelor of Science degree in Business Administration with a concentration in Marketing from the University of Southern California (USC), graduating in the top 10% of my class with a cumulative GPA of 3.6.

As a seasoned information technology professional with a proven track record in NetSuite administration, I bring over 6 years of experience in managing and implementing NetSuite solutions for various clients. My expertise spans across multiple functional modules, including Manufacturing, Wholesale, Financials, and Inventory management, with a strong focus on optimizing business processes and improving operational efficiency. Prior to my current position, I served as a NetSuite Administrator at a leading E-commerce company, where I successfully implemented NetSuite's SuiteCommerce Advanced platform and streamlined their order management and inventory processes. My technical skills are centered around NetSuite, with expertise in SuiteScripting, SuiteFoundation, and RF Smart Commerce. I also have experience with SPS Commerce and have a solid understanding of integration with other systems. I possess excellent problem-solving skills, with the ability to analyze complex business requirements and design customized solutions to meet them. One of my most notable projects was a complete NetSuite overhaul for a mid-sized manufacturer, which involved migrating their existing system to NetSuite 2019 and implementing SuiteCommerce Advanced. This project resulted in a significant reduction in operational costs and improved customer satisfaction. Another notable project was assisting a large distributor in implementing NetSuite's Warehouse Management System (WMS) and optimizing their inventory management processes. I hold a SuiteFoundation Certification and am well-versed in NetSuite best practices and implementation methodologies. I am a certified SuiteFoundation Professional and have a strong passion for staying up-to-date with the latest NetSuite features and technologies. I hold a Bachelor's degree in Business Administration from the University of Utah, with a focus on Information Systems and Technology.
